heroHeadline: Sudan Formally Rejects Berlin Conference; Burhan Announces Largest SAF Command Reshuffle — 21 Generals Reassigned Three Days Before War's Three-Year Anniversary
heroSubtitle: Sudan's ambassador to Germany submitted a formal memorandum on April 12 rejecting the Third Berlin Conference and warning of consequences for sponsoring countries if the April 15 meeting proceeds without Sudanese government participation; the Addis Ababa three-day preparatory meeting concluded with boycotts from the National Forces Alliance and National Forces Coordination still intact; Gen. Burhan simultaneously announced a sweeping reshuffle of 21 SAF generals — the largest command shakeup since the war began — consolidating the appointment of Lt. Gen. Yasser al-Atta as Chief of Staff; UN data confirms 14 million displaced and 33.7 million requiring humanitarian assistance as the conflict enters its fourth year
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026)
